scenario: FEEL needs to determine day of week from date -- e.g., is it Saturday. Wonders function. FEEL day of week function?
question: FEEL day of week function?

options.text: Manual calculation from date components is needed to determine the day of week -- this is overcomplicated. Calculating the day of week from raw date components like year, month, and day requires implementing a Zeller's congruence or Tomohiko Sakamoto algorithm. Using manual arithmetic would be error-prone, harder to maintain, and far less readable than the idiomatic FEEL solution. The built-in function should always be used when available.

md:43-65: the function accepts either a date or a date and time argument and returns an English day name string -- one of "Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday", "Saturday", or "Sunday". " There is no numeric variant in Camunda 8 FEEL. For business day checks use day of week(date) in ["Saturday", "Sunday"]; for scheduling use string comparison against the returned name.

Day of week is determined by subtracting dates from a known epoch -- this does not accurately reflect the behavior. Date subtraction in FEEL yields a duration value, not a day-of-week name. While it is technically possible to compute a day index by subtracting a known reference date and taking the remainder modulo 7, this is far more complex and error-prone than using the built-in day of week function. FEEL provides the temporal function specifically to avoid this kind of manual calculation.

a.text: Incorrect. FEEL day of week: Signature per feel-built-in-functions-temporal.md:47-55: day of week(date: date): string OR day of week(date: date and time): string -- returns English day name (always). Examples (verbatim from :60-65): `feel day of week(date("2019-09-17")) → "Tuesday" day of week(date and time("2019-09-17T12:00:00")) → "Tuesday" day of week(today()) → current English day name For weekday check: feel isWeekend: day of week(date) in ["Saturday", "Sunday"] isWeekday: not(day of week(date) in ["Saturday", "Sunday"]) No numeric variant in C8 FEEL: day of week returns string only (English day name) -- there is NO day of week(date): number overload. For numeric weekday, manual map: if day of week(d) = "Monday" then 1 else if ... else 7 via Decision Table or nested if-then-else. Other temporal functions (per Set 14 Q40): year(date): extract year. month(date): extract month. day(date): extract day. hour(time): extract hour. Use cases: Use case 1: Business hours: feel isBusinessDay: day of week(date) in ["Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday"] Common pattern. Use case 2: Weekend pricing: feel isWeekend: day of week(date) in ["Saturday", "Sunday"] weekendPremium: if isWeekend then amount * 1.5 else amount Use case 3: Schedule lookup: feel dayName: day of week(today()) schedule: schedules[dayName] For locale: Day names typically English in FEEL. For locale-specific names: translate manually. For business day calculation: Pattern: skip weekends: feel // For "next business day" // FEEL doesn't natively know holidays // For weekends only: next: if day of week(today() + duration("P1D")) in ["Saturday", "Sunday"] then today() + duration("P3D") // skip to Monday else today() + duration("P1D") Combine with date arithmetic. For holidays: Native FEEL doesn't know: holiday calendars. Pre-compute externally: pass holidays list as variable. For Decision Tables: Input: day of week Rules: "Monday" → "regular" "Tuesday" → "regular" ... "Saturday" → "weekend" "Sunday" → "weekend" Map day to category. For time portion: feel day of week(date and time("2026-05-20T14:00:00")) → "Wednesday" Date-time argument also supported. For comparing dates: feel isSameDay: date(a) = date(b) Compare date portions. For week number: week of year(date): number per feel-built-in-functions-temporal.md:91-113 -- ISO 8601 week number; verbatim example week of year(date("2019-09-17")) → 38. Useful for: weekly reports. For day of year: day of year(date): number per feel-built-in-functions-temporal.md:67-89 -- Gregorian day number 1-365/366; verbatim example day of year(date("2019-09-17")) → 260. Combinations: feel // Last Friday of month (advanced) ...complex calculation... May require custom logic. For internationalisation: English names: typical. For other locales: map via DT or BKM. feel // Map English to German dayDE: if day of week(date) = "Monday" then "Montag" else if day of week(date) = "Tuesday" then "Dienstag" ... Or via lookup table. For testing: Test all days of week. Weekend / weekday edge cases. For scheduling: Business calendar logic: combines day of week + holidays + business hours. External calendar service: for complex. For Operate visibility: Day extracted from date: visible in variables. Useful for filtering. For audit: Track which day of week operations performed. Compliance: e.g., banking transactions weekday-only. Anti-patterns: Manual calculation: when built-in exists. Hardcoded date logic: brittle. Best practices: Use built-in day of week. Combine with other functions: for business logic. Test edge cases: month boundaries, leap years. External calendar for holidays. For the scenario (is it Saturday): feel isSaturday: day of week(date) = "Saturday" Only string comparison -- no numeric form in C8 FEEL. For full weekend: feel isWeekend: day of week(date) in ["Saturday", "Sunday"] ` For FEEL version: Function name + return type: verify. Camunda 8 specific: docs.
